warnings.message: Global object only used in one function

misra_context.description: Functions and objects should not be defined with external linkage if they are referenced in only one translation unit
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 8.7 (Advisory): Functions and objects should not be defined with external linkage if they are referenced in only one translation unit. This code violates Rule 8.7 because functions and objects should not be defined with external linkage if they are referenced in only one translation unit. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.

misra_context.description: An object should be defined at block scope if its identifier only appears in a single function
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 8.9 (Advisory): An object should be defined at block scope if its identifier only appears in a single function. This code violates Rule 8.9 because an object should be defined at block scope if its identifier only appears in a single function. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.

misra_context.description: An external object or function shall be declared once in one and only one file
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 8.5 (Required): An external object or function shall be declared once in one and only one file. This code violates Rule 8.5 because an external object or function shall be declared once in one and only one file. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.

misra_context.description: Implicit narrowing conversion
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 10.3 (Required): Implicit narrowing conversion. The value of 'u16val' (uint16_t) is assigned to 'u8val' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value (300 → 44), causing data loss and incorrect program behaviour.

misra_context.description: The value of an expression should not be cast to an inappropriate essential type
misra_context.guidelines:
uint32_t wide = 300U;
uint8_t  small = (uint8_t)wide;   /* cast to inappropriate essential type */

misra_context.description: The value of a composite expression shall not be cast to a different essential type category or a wider essential type
misra_context.guidelines:
uint16_t a, b;
uint32_t x = (uint32_t)(a + b);   /* cast composite expression to wider category */

misra_context.description: Both operands of an operator in which the usual arithmetic conversions are performed shall have the same essential type category
The standard input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 21.6 (Required): The standard input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used. This code violates Rule 21.6 because the standard input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used. Restricted standard-library features are disallowed because they reduce determinism, analyzability, or portability.

misra_context.description: The atof, atoi, atol and atoll functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 21.7 (Required): The atof, atoi, atol and atoll functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used. This code violates Rule 21.7 because the atof, atoi, atol and atoll functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used. Restricted standard-library features are disallowed because they reduce determinism, analyzability, or portability.

misra_context.description: There shall be no dead code
A reserved identifier or macro name shall not be declared
misra_context.guidelines: int errno;   /* reserved identifier declared */

misra_context.description: All identifiers used in the controlling expression of #if or #elif shall be #define'd before evaluation
misra_context.guidelines:
#if FEATURE_FLAG
#endif   /* identifier not defined before use */

misra_context.description: Non-Boolean if-controlling expression
The value of an expression and its persistent side effects shall be the same under all permitted evaluation orders
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 13.2 (Required): The value of an expression and its persistent side effects shall be the same under all permitted evaluation orders. This code violates Rule 13.2 because the value of an expression and its persistent side effects shall be the same under all permitted evaluation orders. Side effects in complex expressions make behaviour harder to predict and verify.

misra_context.description: A full expression containing an increment or decrement operator should have no other potential side effects other than that caused by the increment or decrement operator
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 13.3 (Advisory): A full expression containing an increment or decrement operator should have no other potential side effects other than that caused by the increment or decrement operator. This code violates Rule 13.3 because a full expression containing an increment or decrement operator should have no other potential side effects other than that caused by the increment or decrement operator. Side effects in complex expressions make behaviour harder to predict and verify.

misra_context.description: A project shall not contain unreachable code
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 2.1 (Required): A project shall not contain unreachable code. This code violates Rule 2.1 because a project shall not contain unreachable code. Unused, dead, or unreachable constructs increase maintenance cost and can hide real defects.

misra_context.description: There shall be no dead code
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 2.2 (Required): There shall be no dead code. This code violates Rule 2.2 because there shall be no dead code. Unused, dead, or unreachable constructs increase maintenance cost and can hide real defects.

misra_context.description: Functions shall not call themselves, either directly or indirectly
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.2 (Required): Functions shall not call themselves, either directly or indirectly. This code violates Rule 17.2 because functions shall not call themselves, either directly or indirectly. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function shall not be declared implicitly
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.3 (Mandatory): A function shall not be declared implicitly. This code violates Rule 17.3 because a function shall not be declared implicitly. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function parameter should not be modified
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.8 (Advisory): A function parameter should not be modified. This code violates Rule 17.8 because a function parameter should not be modified. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: Non-initialized local variable
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 9.1 (Mandatory): Non-initialized local variable. Variable 'x' is read before being assigned a value. Reading an automatic variable before it is set leads to undefined behaviour and unpredictable results.

misra_context.description: An element of an object shall not be initialized more than once
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 9.4 (Required): An element of an object shall not be initialized more than once. This code violates Rule 9.4 because an element of an object shall not be initialized more than once. Incorrect initialization can leave objects in unexpected states and introduce subtle runtime defects.

misra_context.description: Braces shall be used to indicate and match the structure in the non-zero initialization of arrays and structures
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 9.2 (Required): Braces shall be used to indicate and match the structure in the non-zero initialization of arrays and structures. This code violates Rule 9.2 because braces shall be used to indicate and match the structure in the non-zero initialization of arrays and structures. Incorrect initialization can leave objects in unexpected states and introduce subtle runtime defects.

misra_context.description: Where designated initializers are used to initialize an array object, the size of the array shall be specified explicitly
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 9.5 (Required): Where designated initializers are used to initialize an array object, the size of the array shall be specified explicitly. This code violates Rule 9.5 because where designated initializers are used to initialize an array object, the size of the array shall be specified explicitly. Incorrect initialization can leave objects in unexpected states and introduce subtle runtime defects.

misra_context.description: Implicit narrowing conversion
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 10.3 (Required): Implicit narrowing conversion. The value of 'u16val' (uint16_t) is assigned to 'u8val' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value (300 → 44), causing data loss and incorrect program behaviour.

misra_context.description: The value of an expression should not be cast to an inappropriate essential type
misra_context.guidelines:
uint32_t wide = 300U;
uint8_t  small = (uint8_t)wide;   /* cast to inappropriate essential type */

misra_context.description: The value of a composite expression shall not be cast to a different essential type category or a wider essential type
misra_context.guidelines:
uint16_t a, b;
uint32_t x = (uint32_t)(a + b);   /* cast composite expression to wider category */

misra_context.description: Both operands of an operator in which the usual arithmetic conversions are performed shall have the same essential type category
A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 8.13 (Advisory): A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible. This code violates Rule 8.13 because a pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.

misra_context.description: All declarations of an object or function shall use the same names and type qualifiers
misra_context.guidelines:
extern const int limit;
extern int limit;   /* qualifiers differ */

misra_context.description: An object should be defined at block scope if its identifier only appears in a single function
misra_context.guidelines: int temp_result;   /* only used inside one function */

misra_context.description: Function types shall be in prototype form with named parameters
The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.7 (Required): The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used. This code violates Rule 17.7 because the value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function parameter should not be modified
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.8 (Advisory): A function parameter should not be modified. This code violates Rule 17.8 because a function parameter should not be modified. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function shall not be declared implicitly
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.3 (Mandatory): A function shall not be declared implicitly. This code violates Rule 17.3 because a function shall not be declared implicitly. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: All exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement with an expression
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.4 (Mandatory): All exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement with an expression. This code violates Rule 17.4 because all exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement with an expression. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: Non-Boolean if-controlling expression
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.4 (Required): Non-Boolean if-controlling expression. The controlling expression of the 'if' statement is 'count', an integer, not an essentially Boolean type. Using non-Boolean values as conditions reduces readability and may mask logic errors where the value is non-zero but not the intended condition.

misra_context.description: Controlling expressions shall not be invariant
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.3 (Required): Controlling expressions shall not be invariant. This code violates Rule 14.3 because controlling expressions shall not be invariant. Loop and condition forms should make control flow explicit, safe, and reviewable.

misra_context.description: A loop counter shall not have essentially floating type
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.1 (Required): A loop counter shall not have essentially floating type. This code violates Rule 14.1 because a loop counter shall not have essentially floating type. Loop and condition forms should make control flow explicit, safe, and reviewable.

misra_context.description: Controlling expressions shall not be invariant
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.3 (Required): Controlling expressions shall not be invariant. This code violates Rule 14.3 because controlling expressions shall not be invariant. Loop and condition forms should make control flow explicit, safe, and reviewable.

misra_context.description: A for loop shall be well-formed
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 14.2 (Required): A for loop shall be well-formed. This code violates Rule 14.2 because a for loop shall be well-formed. Loop and condition forms should make control flow explicit, safe, and reviewable.

misra_context.description: The function argument corresponding to a parameter declared to have an array type shall have an appropriate number of elements
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.5 (Advisory): The function argument corresponding to a parameter declared to have an array type shall have an appropriate number of elements. This code violates Rule 17.5 because the function argument corresponding to a parameter declared to have an array type shall have an appropriate number of elements. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 12.2 (Required): The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand. This code violates Rule 12.2 because the right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand. Expression complexity and unsafe shifts can hide errors and make evaluation harder to reason about.
